subtasks.description: Query SQL warehouse to identify top spending publishers by total spend and retrieve their product/category breakdown (mix of products purchased)

Reconcile spending and product mix data with contract terms, pricing history, and market benchmarks to assess negotiation leverage (concentration metrics, switching costs, competitive positioning) for each top publisher

plan.cost_rationale: Two subtasks, not three: spending_query (mid tier) handles multi-table joins across spend and product data with aggregation—moderate complexity for a single SQL call. leverage_assessment (frontier tier) takes the spending results and reconciles them with contract/market data to derive leverage signals (e.g., switching costs, concentration risk, competitive alternatives), then classifies leverage posture—this multi-signal reconciliation with strategic inference requires frontier capability. No separate policy_action needed; leverage assessment is analytical posture-building, not governance approval. No multitool_plan needed; the query does not explicitly invoke the enterprise tool registry.

output.sql: SELECT publisher, product, COUNT(DISTINCT license_id) AS license_count, COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS unique_users, SUM(amount_usd) AS product_spend, SUM(SUM(amount_usd)) OVER (PARTITION BY publisher) AS total_publisher_spend, ROUND(100.0 * SUM(amount_usd) / SUM(SUM(amount_usd)) OVER (PARTITION BY publisher), 2) AS product_pct_of_publisher, COUNT(DISTINCT event_type) AS event_type_diversity, AVG(daily_active_seats) AS avg_daily_active_seats FROM consumption_facts GROUP BY publisher, product ORDER BY total_publisher_spend DESC, publisher, product_spend DESC
